terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: InventoryFlow platform — inventory, billing and point-of-sale management
intro: These Terms of Service (the "Terms") govern access to and use of the InventoryFlow platform (the "Service"), operated by [Company legal name], [legal form], registered under RCCM number [RCCM number], with its registered office at [full address], hereinafter "InventoryFlow", "we".

By creating an account or using the Service, the client business and its users (hereinafter "you", the "User") accept these Terms without reservation. If you do not accept these Terms, you must not use the Service.

sections.heading: Purpose
paragraphs: These Terms set out the conditions under which InventoryFlow makes available to client businesses a software platform for inventory management, billing, point of sale, cash tracking and AI-assisted decision support, together with the parties' respective rights and obligations.
They apply to the exclusion of any other terms, unless a specific contract has been signed between InventoryFlow and the client business.

sections.heading: Definitions
paragraphs: "Business Account" means the primary account created by a client business, to which one or more Sales Points and Users are linked.
"User" means any individual authorized by the client business to access the Service, including its officers, employees, or agents.
"Client Data" means all data entered, imported, or generated by the client business through use of the Service (products, stock, sales, customers, employees, transactions).

sections.heading: Acceptance of the Terms
paragraphs: The Terms are accepted when the Business Account is created, either by ticking the relevant checkbox or through any actual use of the Service.
The client business is responsible for ensuring that the Users it authorizes to access the Service accept the Terms and comply with them.

sections.heading: Account creation and registration
paragraphs: Access to the Service requires creating a Business Account using accurate, complete, and up-to-date information (company name, address, contact details, country, billing currency).
The client business is solely responsible for the confidentiality of its Users' login credentials and for any activity carried out from its Business Account.
Any suspected unauthorized use of the Business Account must be reported to InventoryFlow without delay.

sections.heading: Description of the Service
paragraphs: The Service includes, among others, the following features, the availability of which may vary depending on the subscription plan:
list: Multi-location management of stock, products, suppliers, and packaging;
Invoicing, quotes, and transaction history;
Cash register, expense, and treasury tracking per sales point;
Customer management, including credit and outstanding balance tracking;
AI-assisted analytics and forecasting modules (sales forecasting, reorder suggestions, anomaly detection, customer segmentation);
Management of users, roles, and permissions within the client business.

sections.heading: Subscriptions, pricing and billing
paragraphs: Access to the Service requires subscribing to a paid plan, whose features, number of sales points, and duration are described on the Service's pricing page.
Prices are expressed in the currency chosen by the client business at sign-up and may be revised by InventoryFlow with reasonable prior notice.
A free trial may be offered once per client business, subject to the conditions displayed at sign-up.

sections.heading: Payment and payment methods
paragraphs: Subscriptions may be paid using the payment methods available on the platform, including Mobile Money through partner operators, or by card through a third-party payment provider.
InventoryFlow does not store complete banking data; these operations are processed by third-party payment providers subject to their own terms and security obligations.
Any failure to pay on the due date may, after a reminder, result in the temporary suspension of access to the Service until the situation is resolved.

sections.heading: User obligations
paragraphs: The User undertakes to:
list: Use the Service in accordance with its intended purpose and applicable laws;
Not attempt to circumvent security measures or access data that does not belong to them;
Not use the Service for fraudulent purposes, including falsifying invoices or concealing transactions;
Keep information about their business and Users up to date;
Maintain their own backups of any data they consider critical, in addition to the backups carried out by InventoryFlow.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
paragraphs: InventoryFlow remains the sole owner of all intellectual property rights attached to the Service, its code, interface, and documentation.

The client business retains full ownership of the Client Data it enters into the Service. It grants InventoryFlow a limited, non-exclusive license necessary to host, process, and display that data solely for the purpose of operating the Service.

sections.heading: Personal data protection
paragraphs: The processing of personal data carried out in connection with the Service is described in the Privacy Policy, which forms an integral part of these Terms.
The client business undertakes to inform its own customers and employees whose data is processed through the Service, in accordance with applicable law.

sections.heading: Availability, warranties and liability
paragraphs: InventoryFlow uses reasonable means to ensure the availability, security, and performance of the Service, without however guaranteeing uninterrupted availability.

AI-assisted features (sales forecasts, reorder suggestions, anomaly detection) constitute decision-support tools and cannot replace the client business's own professional judgment; the client business remains solely responsible for its business decisions.

To the extent permitted by applicable law, InventoryFlow's liability may only be engaged in the event of proven fault and shall, in any event, be limited to the amounts paid by the client business under its subscription over the preceding twelve months.

sections.heading: Suspension and termination
paragraphs: InventoryFlow may suspend or terminate access to the Service in the event of a serious breach of these Terms, unresolved non-payment, or fraudulent use, after an unsuccessful formal notice where the situation allows for it.
The client business may terminate its subscription at any time through its account settings; termination takes effect at the end of the current subscription period.
Following termination, Client Data is retained for a reasonable period to allow for export, after which it is deleted in accordance with the Privacy Policy.

sections.heading: Changes to these Terms
paragraphs: InventoryFlow may amend these Terms, in particular to reflect changes to the Service or to applicable law. Users will be notified by appropriate means before the changes take effect.
Continued use of the Service after the amended Terms take effect constitutes acceptance of those Terms.

sections.heading: Governing law and jurisdiction
paragraphs: These Terms are governed by [country to be specified, e.g. Cameroonian / OHADA law], without prejudice to any mandatory provisions that may apply at the client business's place of residence.

Any dispute relating to the validity, interpretation, or performance of these Terms shall, failing an amicable resolution, be submitted to the courts having jurisdiction over InventoryFlow's registered office.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: For any question relating to these Terms, the client business may contact InventoryFlow at legal@inventoryflow.app or by mail at [postal address].

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Processing of personal data within the InventoryFlow Service
intro: InventoryFlow places great importance on protecting the personal data of client businesses, their Users and, where applicable, the individuals whose data is processed through the Service (customers and employees of client businesses).
This Privacy Policy describes the data collected, its purposes, its retention period, and the rights available to the individuals concerned.

sections.heading: Data controller
paragraphs: The controller for data collected through the Service is [Legal name of the company operating InventoryFlow], with its registered office at [full address], reachable at legal@inventoryflow.app.

For Client Data entered into the Service (information relating to the client business's own customers and employees), the client business acts as data controller and InventoryFlow acts as data processor, under the terms specified in the contract between the parties.

sections.heading: Data we collect
paragraphs: Depending on how the Service is used, InventoryFlow may collect the following categories of data:
list: Identification and contact data for the client business and its Users (first and last name, email address, phone number, role);
Connection and usage data for the Service (login logs, IP address, actions performed);
Business data entered into the Service (products, stock, sales, invoices, customers, suppliers, employees);
Limited payment data (payment method used, transaction status), excluding complete banking data, which is processed directly by payment providers;
Technical data required for the Service to operate (cookies, session identifiers).

sections.heading: Purposes of processing
paragraphs: The data collected is used to:
list: Provide, operate, and secure the Service;
Manage the creation and administration of Business Accounts and subscriptions;
Process payments and issue invoices;
Provide support and respond to User requests;
Improve the Service and develop AI features (sales forecasting, reorder suggestions, anomaly detection, customer segmentation), based on aggregated and, where necessary, anonymized data;
Comply with applicable legal and regulatory obligations, in particular accounting and tax obligations.

sections.heading: Legal basis for processing
paragraphs: InventoryFlow's processing activities are based, depending on the case, on the performance of the subscription contract with the client business, on compliance with legal obligations, on InventoryFlow's legitimate interest in securing and improving the Service, or on the consent of the individual concerned where required, in particular for certain non-essential cookies.

sections.heading: Recipients and processors
paragraphs: Data is accessible to InventoryFlow's authorized teams, strictly within the scope of their duties, as well as to technical service providers necessary to deliver the Service, including hosting providers, payment providers (Mobile Money operators, card payment providers), and communication providers.
These providers are selected for the security and confidentiality guarantees they offer and only use the data for the purposes defined by InventoryFlow.

sections.heading: Data transfers
paragraphs: Data is primarily hosted and processed in data centers located in [hosting region to be specified]. Where a transfer outside this region is necessary, in particular with certain technical service providers, InventoryFlow ensures that appropriate safeguards are put in place in accordance with applicable law.

sections.heading: Retention period
paragraphs: Client Data is retained for the entire duration of the Business Account, then for a reasonable period after termination to allow the client business to export it, before deletion or anonymization.
Data required to comply with legal obligations, in particular accounting and tax obligations, is retained for the applicable statutory periods.

sections.heading: Data security
paragraphs: InventoryFlow implements reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect data against unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or disclosure, including encryption of communications, role-based access control, and logging of sensitive actions.

In the event of a data breach likely to create a risk for the individuals concerned, InventoryFlow will notify the affected client businesses and, where applicable, the competent authorities, within the timeframes set out by applicable law.

sections.heading: Cookies and trackers
paragraphs: The Service uses cookies strictly necessary for it to function (authentication, security, language preferences) as well as, where applicable, audience measurement cookies.
Users may configure their browser to refuse certain cookies, although this may affect the proper functioning of the Service.

sections.heading: Your rights
paragraphs: Subject to the conditions set out in applicable law, every individual concerned has the following rights:
list: Right of access to their data and to obtain a copy;
Right to rectify inaccurate or incomplete data;
Right to erasure, within the limits of InventoryFlow's legal retention obligations;
Right to portability of the data they provided;
Right to object to and to request restriction of processing, in the cases provided for by applicable law.

sections.heading: Children's data
paragraphs: The Service is intended for professional use and is not designed to be used by minors. InventoryFlow does not knowingly collect data relating to minors.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: InventoryFlow may amend this Privacy Policy to reflect changes to the Service, its practices, or applicable law. The last update date appears at the top of this document.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: For any question relating to this Privacy Policy or to exercise your rights, you may contact InventoryFlow at legal@inventoryflow.app or by mail at [postal address]. These rights may also be exercised directly with the client business when it acts as the controller of your data.
